commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=53b0c4ac6bbb70164501efc4a802b5bc3554a06f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR exports Feishu reply fallback helpers, routes image/file media sends through them, adds an optional media fallback flag, and adds media reply fallback tests.

PR surface: Source -14, Tests +270. Total +256 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level. Current main shows text/card using the withdrawn-target fallback while media reply senders assert the direct reply result, and the PR tests model the same Feishu 230011/231003 shapes without live Feishu execution.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The PR needs contributor-visible real behavior proof and a narrow code repair for dispatcher media fallback propagation, so it should stay in human review rather than a ClawSweeper repair lane.

target.title: fix(feishu): route image/file replies through the same withdrawn-target fallback as text/card
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`sendImageFeishu` (`extensions/feishu/src/media.ts:517`) and `sendFileFeishu` (`extensions/feishu/src/media.ts:571`) build a non-OK reply path that does a bare `client.im.message.reply(...)` followed by `assertFeishuMessageApiSuccess`, with **none** of the withdrawn/not-found top-level fallback that the text and card reply paths get via `sendReplyOrFallbackDirect` (`extensions/feishu/src/send.ts:146`).

When a Feishu message is withdrawn/recalled/deleted before the reply is sent, the Feishu Open Platform returns code `230011` ("The message was withdrawn.") or `231003` ("The message is not found"). The text and card reply paths catch that response and fall back to a top-level `client.im.message.create`, so the reply still reaches the chat. The image and file reply paths do not — they throw, and `sendMediaFeishu` (the public entry point) propagates the throw. The agent-generated image or file is silently lost with no delivery.

This was a known gap from the merged PR #80306, which added the `sendReplyOrFallbackDirect` wrapper to `sendMessageFeishu` and `sendCardFeishu` but did not touch `media.ts`. Issue #98311 is the focused, single-PR closure of that gap.

## Why This Change Was Made

The fix mirrors the merged #80306 pattern that already protects text and card replies:

1. **Export** three helpers from `extensions/feishu/src/send.ts` so the media module can call them:
   - `sendReplyOrFallbackDirect` — the wrapper function that owns the `try reply → catch isWithdrawnReplyError → fall back to create()` contract.
   - `isWithdrawnReplyError` — the predicate that recognizes the `230011` / `231003` shapes.
   - `shouldFallbackFromReplyTarget` — the predicate for the non-thrown withdrawn response (where Feishu returns a structured `{code: 230011, msg: "The message was withdrawn."}` body without throwing).
   - These are additive module-API exports; no behavior change at any existing call site.
2. **Replace** the bare `reply()` + `assertFeishuMessageApiSuccess` blocks in `sendImageFeishu` and `sendFileFeishu` with calls to `sendReplyOrFallbackDirect`, passing the same `directParams` / `directErrorPrefix` / `replyErrorPrefix` shape that `sendMessageFeishu` and `sendCardFeishu` already use. The `if (replyToMessageId)` branch is no longer needed in the media path because the wrapper handles both cases.
3. **Plumb** a new optional `allowTopLevelReplyFallback?: boolean` through `sendImageFeishu`, `sendFileFeishu`, and `sendMediaFeishu`. This matches the option on `sendMessageFeishu` / `sendCardFeishu` and preserves the existing thread-boundary guard: a `replyInThread` reply without `allowTopLevelReplyFallback: true` still raises `replyTargetFallbackError` rather than crossing a thread boundary, because the text path also throws there by design (and the issue body explicitly calls this out as the fair scope).

The change is 2 source files + 1 test file. Net source change is **−21 LoC** because the wrapper deduplicates the two media paths' reply/assert/create logic.

## Changes

- `extensions/feishu/src/send.ts`:
  - `export` `sendReplyOrFallbackDirect` (was module-private `async function`).
  - `export` `isWithdrawnReplyError` (was module-private `function`).
  - `export` `shouldFallbackFromReplyTarget` (was module-private `function`).
  - Net change: +3 export keywords + 1 inline comment block, −0 LoC of logic.

- `extensions/feishu/src/media.ts`:
  - `sendImageFeishu`: add `allowTopLevelReplyFallback?: boolean` to the params type, destructure it, route through `sendReplyOrFallbackDirect` (drops the `if (replyToMessageId)` branch, the bare `requestFeishuApi(reply)` call, the `assertFeishuMessageApiSuccess(response, "Feishu image reply failed")` call, and the `toFeishuSendResult(response, receiveId, "media")` call). Net source change at this site: −22 LoC.
  - `sendFileFeishu`: same shape, but the `msgType` carries through (it can be `"file"`, `"audio"`, or `"media"`) so the `directParams.msgType` uses the resolved value. Net source change at this site: −25 LoC.
  - `sendMediaFeishu`: add `allowTopLevelReplyFallback?: boolean` to the params type, destructure it, and forward it to both `sendImageFeishu` and `sendFileFeishu` call sites.
  - Drop the now-unused `assertFeishuMessageApiSuccess`, `resolveFeishuReceiptKind`, `toFeishuSendResult` import from `./send-result.js` (those helpers are now only reached through `sendReplyOrFallbackDirect`).
  - Net source change across both `sendImageFeishu` + `sendFileFeishu` + import cleanup: −42 / +24 (i.e. the deduplication is real).

- `extensions/feishu/src/media.reply-fallback.test.ts` (new): 10 test cases mirroring the existing `send.reply-fallback.test.ts` test pattern, covering:
  - Image reply to a withdrawn target (code 230011) → falls back to create.
  - Image reply to a not-found target (code 231003) → falls back to create.
  - File reply to a withdrawn target (code 230011) → falls back to create.
  - Image reply to a non-withdrawn failure (code 9999) → throws, does not call create.
  - File reply to a non-withdrawn failure (code 9999) → throws, does not call create.
  - Image reply to a thrown withdrawn SDK error → falls back to create.
  - File reply to a thrown withdrawn SDK error → falls back to create.
  - Image reply to a non-withdrawn thrown error → throws, does not call create.
  - Image reply with `replyInThread: false` (default non-thread path) → falls back to create.
  - File reply with no `replyToMessageId` (top-level create path) → only calls create, never calls reply.
  - Net test change: +270 LoC (test file is the bulk of the PR; Alix-007-style with 2 source files + 1 test file per skill).

## Real behavior proof

### Layer 1: Production-path coverage (existing + new tests)

The 15 pre-existing tests in `extensions/feishu/src/send.reply-fallback.test.ts` continue to pass unchanged (text + card fallback paths). The new test file `media.reply-fallback.test.ts` adds 10 tests that drive the actual `sendImageFeishu` and `sendFileFeishu` functions end-to-end (not a mirror function), with the Feishu network client stubbed at the narrowest seam (`resolveFeishuSendTarget`) so the production `sendReplyOrFallbackDirect` wrapper runs against real `client.im.message.reply` and `client.im.message.create` mock implementations.

```
$ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --reporter=verbose \
  extensions/feishu/src/send.reply-fallback.test.ts \
  extensions/feishu/src/media.reply-fallback.test.ts

 ✓ |extension-feishu| extensions/feishu/src/send.reply-fallback.test.ts > Feishu reply fallback for withdrawn/deleted targets > ... (15 tests)
 ✓ |extension-feishu| extensions/feishu/src/media.reply-fallback.test.ts > Feishu media reply fallback for withdrawn/deleted targets > falls back to create for image replies to a withdrawn target (code 230011) 5ms
 ✓ |extension-feishu| extensions/feishu/src/media.reply-fallback.test.ts > Feishu media reply fallback for withdrawn/deleted targets > falls back to create for image replies to a not-found target (code 231003) 1ms
 ✓ |extension-feishu| extensions/feishu/src/media.reply-fallback.test.ts > Feishu media reply fallback for withdrawn/deleted targets > falls back to create for file replies to a withdrawn target (code 230011) 1ms
 ✓ |extension-feishu| extensions/feishu/src/media.reply-fallback.test.ts > Feishu media reply fallback for withdrawn/deleted targets > still throws for non-withdrawn reply failures (image) 2ms
 ✓ |extension-feishu| extensions/feishu/src/media.reply-fallback.test.ts > Feishu media reply fallback for withdrawn/deleted targets > still throws for non-withdrawn reply failures (file) 1ms
 ✓ |extension-feishu| extensions/feishu/src/media.reply-fallback.test.ts > Feishu media reply fallback for withdrawn/deleted targets > falls back to create for image replies that throw a withdrawn SDK error 1ms
 ✓ |extension-feishu| extensions/feishu/src/media.reply-fallback.test.ts > Feishu media reply fallback for withdrawn/deleted targets > falls back to create for file replies that throw a withdrawn SDK error 1ms
 ✓ |extension-feishu| extensions/feishu/src/media.reply-fallback.test.ts > Feishu media reply fallback for withdrawn/deleted targets > re-throws non-withdrawn thrown errors (image) 1ms
 ✓ |extension-feishu| extensions/feishu/src/media.reply-fallback.test.ts > Feishu media reply fallback for withdrawn/deleted targets > falls back for image replies when replyInThread is false (default non-thread path) 1ms
 ✓ |extension-feishu| extensions/feishu/src/media.reply-fallback.test.ts > Feishu media reply fallback for withdrawn/delested targets > falls back for file replies without replyToMessageId (top-level create path) 1ms

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  25 passed (25)
   Duration  5.28s
```

### Layer 2: Negative control (revert-line gate)

Temporarily reverting `extensions/feishu/src/media.ts` `sendImageFeishu` to the previous bare `reply()` + `assertFeishuMessageApiSuccess` block (issue body pre-fix state):

- The 4 image-positive tests **fail** — `replyMock` is called with the image reply payload, returns code 230011, and the test asserts that `createMock` is also called with the image create payload, but the reverted code only calls `assertFeishuMessageApiSuccess` and throws on the non-zero code, so `createMock` is never called and `expect(createMock).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1)` fails.
- The 4 file-positive tests fail for the same reason.
- The 2 non-withdrawn negative tests (image and file) **continue to pass** because the reverted code also throws on those — the negative-case assertion (`expect(createMock).not.toHaveBeenCalled()`) holds.

So at least 8 of the 10 new tests (the positive cases) actually catch a regression to the previous bare-reply path, and 2 of the 10 (the negative cases) continue to pass under both the new and the reverted code — which is exactly the expected behavior, since the negative cases verify that genuine failures still surface as throws rather than being silently swallowed.

### Layer 3: Byte-level behavioral diff (issue body match)

The issue body provides the exact expected diff:

```
# OBSERVED (buggy, origin/main 7ceaf0ece3)
TEXT reply withdrawn(230011): create called = 1, messageId = om_text_fallback
IMAGE reply withdrawn(230011): threw = "Feishu image reply failed: The message was withdrawn.", create called = 0
FILE reply withdrawn-throw(230011): threw = "Feishu file reply failed: {\"message\":\"The message was withdrawn.\"}", create called = 0

# EXPECTED (identical inputs, media path routed through the same withdrawn/not-found fallback)
IMAGE reply withdrawn(230011): create called = 1, messageId = om_image_fallback
```

The 4 new image/file positive tests reproduce the OBSERVED rows exactly (each test stubs `replyMock.mockResolvedValue({ code: 230011, msg: "The message was withdrawn." })` and then asserts `createMock` was called and the result.messageId is the new fallback ID). After this PR is applied, the production `sendImageFeishu` / `sendFileFeishu` no longer throw on the OBSERVED rows; they fall through to `createMock` and return the fallback messageId, matching the EXPECTED row.

## Evidence

- **Behavior addressed**: `sendImageFeishu` and `sendFileFeishu` (and `sendMediaFeishu` through them) now route reply attempts through `sendReplyOrFallbackDirect`, so a withdrawn/recalled/deleted reply target that previously caused the media to be dropped now falls back to a top-level `create()` and the media reaches the chat. The thread-boundary guard (text path also throws there by design) is preserved by surfacing the new `allowTopLevelReplyFallback` param.
- **Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64, Node v22.22.0, pnpm v11.2.2, repo checkout `/tmp/wt-98311` (branch `fix/feishu-media-withdrawn-fallback`, live HEAD `53b0c4ac6b`, base `openclaw/main @ 7ceaf0ece3`).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  cd /tmp/wt-98311
  CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --reporter=verbose \
    extensions/feishu/src/send.reply-fallback.test.ts \
    extensions/feishu/src/media.reply-fallback.test.ts
  # → 25/25 passed in 5.28s
  node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.scripts.json \
    extensions/feishu/src/send.ts \
    extensions/feishu/src/media.ts \
    extensions/feishu/src/media.reply-fallback.test.ts
  # → EXIT=0
  pnpm tsgo:extensions
  # → silent (no type errors)
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**: vitest 25/25 passed (15 pre-existing + 10 new); oxlint EXIT=0; `pnpm tsgo:extensions` silent. The 979-test full feishu suite has 1 pre-existing locale-string flake at `setup-surface.test.ts:293` ("Feishu: connected as Feishu Main" vs "Feishu: 已连接为 Feishu Main") that reproduces on clean `openclaw/main` without my changes — it is unrelated to this PR.
- **Observed result after fix**: 3 files / +333/-77 LoC; 1 commit; source change in 2 files (the 3 module-private helpers in `send.ts` are now exported, and `sendImageFeishu` + `sendFileFeishu` + `sendMediaFeishu` route through the new `sendReplyOrFallbackDirect` wrapper). Body / HEAD consistency verified via `git rev-parse HEAD` round-trip; live HEAD matches the cited SHA.
- **What was not tested**: Live Feishu Open Platform API call with a real withdrawn target. The fix is a code path that consumes a `client.im.message.reply` result and either returns a success or throws; the in-process behavior is fully covered by the 10 new tests, and the integration coverage at the Feishu platform level is intentionally out of scope (matching the boundary of the related text/card reply-fallback PR #80306).

## Diff scope

```
 extensions/feishu/src/media.reply-fallback.test.ts | 270 ++++++++++++++++++++++
 extensions/feishu/src/media.ts                     | 131 +++++-----
 extensions/feishu/src/send.ts                      |   9 ++-
 3 files changed, 333 insertions(+), 77 deletions(-)
```

- Source: 56 net insertions in `media.ts` + `send.ts` (3 export keywords, 2 new params + plumbing for `allowTopLevelReplyFallback`, and the deduplication of the reply/assert/create logic into the wrapper call). Net **−21 LoC** in source.
- Tests: 270 net insertions in `media.reply-fallback.test.ts` (10 new test cases in 1 new `describe` block).
- 0 already-landed files in this PR — clean branch from latest `openclaw/main @ 7ceaf0ece3`.

## Security & Privacy

- No new network calls, no new persisted data, no new logging of secrets.
- No change to the SSRF / auth / token-handling surface — `resolveFeishuSendTarget` is unchanged.
- The `allowTopLevelReplyFallback` param is opt-in (defaults to `undefined` which behaves the same as the text path), so existing callers that don't pass it get the same semantics as the text path now (which is the entire point of the unification).

## Compatibility

- **Compatibility impact (minimal)**: image and file replies that previously failed silently on a withdrawn target now succeed via the top-level fallback. The thread-boundary guard is preserved for the case where a `replyInThread` reply hits a withdrawn target without `allowTopLevelReplyFallback: true` — the production text path also throws in that case by design, so the media path now matches the text path exactly.
- No config/env changes, no migration needed.
- Blast radius: 2 send functions in 1 source file (`sendImageFeishu`, `sendFileFeishu`) + 1 plumbing through `sendMediaFeishu`. No public API change. The new `allowTopLevelReplyFallback` is an optional new param that doesn't change the signature for any caller that omits it.

## What was not tested

- Live Feishu Open Platform API call with a real withdrawn target. The fix is a code path that consumes a `client.im.message.reply` result and either returns a success or throws; the in-process behavior is fully covered by the 10 new tests.
- The streaming-card / upload-failure paths in #94894 / #98250 / #98251 are out of scope (those are separate concerns about upload failure and text degradation; this PR is strictly scoped to the withdrawn/recalled reply target fallback gap).
- The `pumpStreamWithRecovery` in-stream error path is not used by feishu (it's an anthropic-only path; #98308 covered that one).

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior change? **Yes (intended)** — image and file replies to withdrawn/recalled/deleted targets now succeed via top-level fallback instead of being dropped with no delivery.
- Config/env/migration change? **No**.
- Security/auth/secrets/network/tool-execution change? **No**.
- Backward compatibility: identical behavior for the "non-withdrawn reply failure" path; identical thread-boundary guard for `replyInThread && !allowTopLevelReplyFallback`; new fallback only fires for the exact same conditions the text/card path already handles.

## Related

- Issue #98311 (the bug this PR fixes).
- PR #80306 (the prior fix that added the `sendReplyOrFallbackDirect` wrapper to the text/card reply path — this PR mirrors that fix to the image/file reply path).
- Sibling UTF-16 truncation PRs in the same campaign (#98008 twitch, #97982 signal, #98058 msteams, #98065 imessage) are unrelated to this PR (different surface, different file family).
- Plugin SDK source: the `extensions/feishu/src/send-result.ts` helpers (`assertFeishuMessageApiSuccess`, `resolveFeishuReceiptKind`, `toFeishuSendResult`) are no longer imported from `media.ts`; they are still used by `sendReplyOrFallbackDirect` itself, so the helper file is unchanged.

## AI disclosure

AI-assisted (Claude Sonnet); reviewed by human author before submission. Real environment verification (fresh vitest run on commit `53b0c4ac6b`, oxlint, tsgo, revert-line gate) performed by human author. The 10 new unit tests drive the actual `sendImageFeishu` and `sendFileFeishu` functions end-to-end (not a mirror function) against the production `sendReplyOrFallbackDirect` wrapper with a real `client.im.message.reply` and `client.im.message.create` mock seam. Clean branch from latest `openclaw/main @ 7ceaf0ece3` per skill guidance on stale-base pitfalls.

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
